name: Aloe Vera
careinstructions: A beautiful, popular and low maintenance succulent. It's best to leave this plant alone for the most part, as overwatering is typically more damaging than underwatering. Requires cactusspecific soil blends. Container size is unimporant.  The small tap root grows well in large or small containers. Fertilizer or plant food is rarely necessary for an Aloe Vera to thrive. Aloe, when healthy, does tend to produce lots of new leaves.  It can be necessary to trim off the unhealthy or dead leaves. To do this, use a sterilized knife and carefully cut the base of the leaf, paying special attention to not damage healthy leaves.

name: Chrysanthemum
careinstructions: Chrysanthemums work as well indoors as outdoors.  They require a large amount of direct sunlight, and their soil shouldn't be allowed to dry.

name: Anthurium
careinstructions: Anthurium require well-drained soil and their soil should be allowed to dry between watering. Take care not to over-water though, as this can cause root rot.

name: Inchplant
careinstructions: Inchplant prefers well-drained soil, and take care not to over-water, as this can cause root rot. Contact with sap from this plant can cause skin irritation.  Direct sunlught can cause leaves to wither.

name: Philodendron
careinstructions: This plant requires loose, rich soil, a warm environment, and a large amount of indirect, bright light

name: English Ivy
careinstructions: English Ivy requires some maintenance, requiring some fertilizer and a relatively cool, humid environment.  This plant also requires well-drained soil.  English Ivy will tolerate a variety of light conditions but will thrive in moderate-light, indirect light. This plant will require some extra maintenance to prevent pests (mites).

name: Dragon Tree
careinstructions: A very hardy plant, can tolerate a variety of conditions, but do well in a warm, humid environment with lots of indirect light.

name: Boston Fern
careinstructions: This fern requires frequent but minimal watering. It's best to keeo your Boston fern lightly misted so that the soil is consistently moist, without being soaked.

name: Gardenia
careinstructions: Quite a challenging plant to make happy.  This plant will require close attention to light, temperature, humidity and pest control.  Gardenia requires a high degree of humidity and about half a day's exposure to direct, bright light.  Keep in mind that Gardenia does not like to be moved frequently.

name: Aazalea
careinstructions: Azaleas can be tricky to get to flower indoors.  These plants require cooler temperatures while being mindful of frost, bright and indirect sunlight, and consistently moist (but not soaked) soil.

name: Pinstripe Calathea
careinstructions: Pinstripe Calathea are very sensitive to temperature fluctuation and require a lot of bright, indirct sunlight.  Direct sun will damage the leaves.  They also require humidity, and it is very important to keep their soil moist at all times.

name: Weeping Fig
careinstructions: Weeping Fig is an interesting plant in that it can tolerate a wide variety of light and moisture conditions, it must be acclimatized gradually.  This plant prefers a sunny, humid environment and many people find when they bring this plant home from a nursary that it will start losing foliage quickly.

name: Miniature Rose
careinstructions: Miniature roses are beautiful but will wither if they do not get enough sunlight.  It's important to keep their soil consistently damp without ever soaking.  It's also very important to not get water on the leaves of this plant.

name: Catasetum Orchid
